item.title: Lzon.ca. A personal blog, by a programmer and IT expert.
item.description: A personal blog, by a programmer and IT expert. Essays, Articles, Guides, and Recipes. As well as Code, Quotes, and Links.

item.title: Odd gestures in public
item.description: Apple’s watch fulfilled the prophecy made by Brazilian pop duo Claudinho e Buchecha back in 1998 — and suddenly, I could control my calendar — and many other things — without using my hands.

item.title: Bringing Claude Code Into Neovim
item.description: I live in two places: a terminal running Claude Code and Neovim. For months I bounced between them. Edit in Neovim, alt-tab to the terminal, paste a file path, ask Claude something, alt-tab back, apply the change by hand. The friction was small but constant, and constant friction is the kind that wears a groove in your day.

item.title: A Deadline Is a Constraint, Not a Threat
item.description: A deadline becomes a threat when date, scope, and quality are all fixed at once but a useful constraint when it forces a team to decide what must be true first. This post is about why even autonomous teams get stuck, what separates the "smallest coherent change" from the "smallest visible feature" and why good leadership doesn't apply pressure from the outside but builds a better constraint from the inside. A deadline worth having doesn't reduce quality; it reduces ambiguity.

item.title: Your Prime Is Not Youth, Money, or Status. It Is Something More Human
item.description: Your Prime Is Not Youth, Money, or Status. It Is Something More Human

item.title: I like Claude Desktop, so I created my own
item.description: Building my own Claude Desktop with Tauri, GLM-5.2, the Claude Agent SDK, and Apple container machines

item.title: Clean Architecture protects the happy zone | Christian Rackerseder
item.description: Clean Architecture is not only about drawing onions or creating folders. It is about protecting pure application logic from the messy outside world.

item.title: Most rewrites serve the engineer, not the business
item.description: The urge to rewrite working code usually serves the engineer, not the business. A test for when a rewrite is worth it, and what AI does and doesn't change.
item.author: (Anatoliy Babushka)

item.title: Grief, Growth, and my Future as a Programmer
item.description: As AI transforms software development, this is my honest account of moving from fear and resistance to adaptation and the mindset shifts I’m making to stay relevant and fulfilled.

item.title: stop asking writers about "AI"
item.description: Every time I tell someone new that I’m a writer, they pause for a moment, then ask the same question: “So, what do you think about AI?” This question has been sapping my motivation to write for years.

item.title: There is no 'middle' between working and owning
item.description: Do you see yourself as a worker? In this article, a tech worker pierces through the myth of the ‘middle class’.

item.title: Minor contagious metadata corruption in exiv2
item.description: All rituals restricted. All rites reserved.

item.title: Small acts of defiance
item.description: A benign rebellion.

item.title: localhost is disappearing
item.description: Why localhost is becoming the wrong default for agentic software development.
item.author: (Jordan Fulghum)

item.title: why agent-to-agent economy will explode — lucianocccc
item.description: 6 giugno 2028

item.title: Preface | Causality-Oriented Architecture Notes
item.description: About Software Engineering

item.title: The Last People Who Know How It Works · unix.foo
item.description: You only ever know the things you can lose to.

item.title: Claude Science beta | Claude by Anthropic
item.description: Claude Science is your AI workbench for scientific research. Works through your research like a skilled scientist, running the analysis and tracing every step. Spend less time stitching pipelines together, and more time on the science.

item.title: Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.description: Google DeepMind

item.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5
item.description: Our most agentic Sonnet yet, with top-tier intelligence for coding and everyday professional work.

item.title: The Post-COVID Decline in the Labor Share - Liberty Street Economics
item.description: A look at the decline of the labor share of income that occurred post-COVID, comparing its dynamics to declines in earlier periods.

item.title: Claude Code Is Steganographically Marking Requests
item.description: I inspected Claude Code for privacy reasons and found hidden system prompt markers based on API base URL and timezone.

item.title: VS Code Running in a Docker Container with X11 Forwarding | no dogma blog
item.description: Forwarding the screen from Docker Desktop containers in Linux is much more difficult than it should be. In an earlier post, I gave an example of forwarding xeyes, but here is something a little more practical - running VS Code in a container.

item.title: Waag | European digital ID wallets are a gift to Google and Apple
item.description: European governments are rolling out digital identity wallets, which are to be used by citizens to access services, and to verify their age online. There is a serious problem with this: these wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple.

item.title: The Dating App Plot Device
item.description: I've always been interested in how dating apps work. You really only have two choices if you want to get in the business.
